title: Pleasure! why thus desert the heart
content:
Pleasure! why thus desert the heart
    In its spring-tide?
    I could have seen her, I could part,
    And but have sigh'd!

    O'er every youthful charm to stray,
    To gaze, to touch....
    Pleasure! why take so much away,
    Or give so much?
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter Savage Landor, titled "Pleasure! why thus desert the heart", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
